candidates.title: Evidence-gated research factory for Develop a policy-gated toolchain installation protocol for autonomous Linux research nodes that prevents unsafe host installs and records reproducible tool evidence.
candidates.technicalField: Autonomous open-source research systems, evidence scoring, and controlled publication workflows.
candidates.problem: Autonomous research systems can generate plausible artifacts without enough evidence, tests, or publication safety controls.
candidates.proposedSolution: Use a deterministic factory that plans research questions, discovers and reads public sources, builds a feature matrix, maps candidate novelty gaps, selects invention candidates, generates Open Invention missions, and gates publication on evidence strength.
differentiators: Evidence-bound source discovery and source reading
candidates.expectedPrototype: A deterministic scorer that ranks factory candidates and blocks weak evidence.
candidates.expectedTests: Tests for evidence hash binding, scoring caps, and curated release packaging.

topCounterEvidence.overlapDescription: Examples and tests can inform prototype expectations for research agents.
topCounterEvidence.whyItWeakensNovelty: Source-supported overlap means the selected candidate must not present this feature as new without a clearer differentiator.
topCounterEvidence.whyItMayNotFullyCoverCandidate: candidate novelty axis: compare this source claim against Sovryn's replayable evidence gate.
topCounterEvidence.requiredFollowUpSearch: Review the concrete source and search for additional implementations before public release.
topCounterEvidence.recommendedAction: Keep this as counter-evidence in the defensive publication dossier and use careful non-legal language.

invalidationConditions: A concrete source shows the same source-card binding, replay, prototype execution, and curated publication gate combination.
Prototype tests fail to distinguish weak evidence from ready evidence.
strengtheningExperiments: Run additional source reads and verify counter-evidence does not fully cover the candidate.

candidates.title: Source matrix novelty-gap scorer
candidates.technicalField: Research evidence analysis and reproducibility tooling.
candidates.problem: Research automation often lacks a conservative bridge from sources to novelty-risk notes.
candidates.proposedSolution: Convert source discovery and source reading evidence into feature rows, candidate novelty axes, and conservative novelty-risk hints.
differentiators: Legal conclusions are explicitly out of scope
candidates.expectedPrototype: A small library that turns source readings into feature-matrix rows and gap scores.
candidates.expectedTests: Fixture tests for concrete sources, query links, and mock placeholders.

invalidationConditions: Existing tools already map source cards to novelty-risk rows with comparable evidence hashes.
strengtheningExperiments: Add concrete source cards from at least two source types and rerun matrix scoring.

candidates.title: Curated public evidence curator
candidates.technicalField: Open-source release engineering and evidence publication.
candidates.problem: Public research releases can leak raw logs or local execution metadata while trying to be transparent.
candidates.proposedSolution: Create a release/public packager that emits only redacted summaries for source discovery, readings, features, gaps, candidates, and scores.
differentiators: No raw stdout/stderr in public release
candidates.expectedPrototype: A packager that copies curated summaries and rejects raw command logs.
candidates.expectedTests: Tests for curated file allowlist and secret scan failures.

invalidationConditions: Existing release systems already provide equivalent curated evidence packages for autonomous research factories.
strengtheningExperiments: Package public evidence and scan for raw logs, paths, and secrets.
